content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA consisting of NNN non-negative integers.

It is guaranteed that AAA contains **at least two distinct** elements.

You are also given a non-negative integer XXX.

For each YYY (0≤Y≤X)(0 \leq Y \leq X)(0≤Y≤X), we create an array BBB of size NNN such that Bi=Ai∣YB_i = A_i | YBi​=Ai​∣Y, where ∣|∣ denotes the bitwise OR operation.

Find the **largest** integer Y(Y≤X)Y(Y \leq X)Y(Y≤X) such that the array BBB contains **at least two distinct** elements.

**Function Description:**You are given a function named **`maximizeXor`** that you must complete.

The function has the following parameters:


**`a`** – a vector of `N` non-negative integers
**`x`** – a non-negative integer representing the upper bound for `Y`
The function must **return this maximum valid value of `Y`**.

The input and output formats given below are only if you want to test using custom inputs.
constraints: The sum of N over all test cases does not exceed 2·10^5
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines. The first line contains two integers N and X — the size of the array and the maximum allowed value of Y. The second line contains N space‑separated non‑negative integers A1, A2, …, AN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the largest integer Y (0 ≤ Y ≤ X) such that after replacing every Ai by (Ai | Y) the resulting array contains at least two distinct values.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 1: For Y=5, B=[1|5,1|5,2|5]=[5,5,7], which has 2 distinct elements.
Thus, this is the largest value of Y.
Test case 2: For Y=10, B=[4|10,5|10,2|10,3|10,5|10]=[14,15,10,11,15], which has 4 distinct elements.
Thus, this is the largest value of Y.
Test case 3: For Y=1, B=[1|1,1|1,3|1]=[1,1,3], which has 2 distinct elements.
For Y=2, B=[1|2,1|2,3|2]=[3,3,3], which has 1 distinct element only.

solution.approach: For a fixed Y the condition "array B has at least two distinct elements" can be checked in O(N) by computing the first value v = A0 | Y and verifying whether any other Ai | Y differs from v. Observe that if Y1 ≤ Y2 (bitwise) then every Ai|Y1 ≤ Ai|Y2, therefore increasing Y can only make distinct values merge, never create new ones. Consequently the predicate P(Y): "B has ≥2 distinct values" is monotone decreasing with Y. Hence the set of Y that satisfy P is a prefix [0, Ymax]. We can binary‑search the largest Y in [0, X] for which P(Y) holds. Each test case needs O(N·log X) time (log X ≤ 30) and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N·log X) per test case (≈ 30·N), total O((sum N)·log X).
